No Poverty.reason: The course content does not explicitly or implicitly address poverty eradication, social protection, or economic resources for the poor. While data science can indirectly support economic development, there's no direct link to poverty reduction in the syllabus.

Zero Hunger.reason: There is no mention of food security, nutrition, sustainable agriculture, or related topics in the course description or schedule.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course syllabus does not contain any content related to health, well-being, disease prevention, or healthcare services.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3). It also focuses on 'data science core techniques' and 'problem solving' to equip students with relevant skills for employment (Target 4.4).
evidence: 科目名稱：資料科學核心技術專題
培養問題解決能力 (Students will acquire practical professional skills - problem solving.)

Gender Equality.reason: The course syllabus does not include any specific content or objectives related to gender equality, women's empowerment, or addressing gender disparities.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: There is no evidence in the course description or learning outcomes that relates to water quality, sanitation, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course content does not address energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency in any capacity.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course promotes economic productivity through 'AI intelligent era technology applications' and 'machine learning' (Target 8.2). It also strengthens financial institutions via 'financial industry data application trends and risk management' (Target 8.10).
evidence: 金融業數據應用趨勢與風險管理

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course is heavily focused on upgrading technological capabilities and fostering innovation (Target 9.5) through topics like 'AI intelligent era technology applications', 'machine learning', 'big data', and 'artificial intelligence basic applications'.
evidence: 大數據及人工智慧之基礎應用

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course covers 'International General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)', which can indirectly contribute to reducing inequalities by ensuring fair data practices (Target 10.3). 'Financial industry data application trends and risk management' can improve financial market regulation (Target 10.5).
evidence: 國際一般資料保護規範GDPR
金融業數據應用趨勢與風險管理

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course content does not relate to urban development, housing, transport, or community planning.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The core competency 'understanding and identifying ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues' provides an inferred link to promoting sustainable practices (Target 12.6) and efficient resource use (Target 12.2).
evidence: 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題的能力 (Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.)

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability issues' are mentioned, the course does not specifically address climate change, its impacts, or mitigation/adaptation strategies.

Life Below Water.reason: There is no content in the syllabus related to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable use of marine resources.

Life on Land.reason: The course does not cover terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, deforestation, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course directly supports Target 16.6 (effective institutions) through 'data governance' and 'risk management'. It also addresses Target 16.10 (protect freedoms) via 'International General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)'.
evidence: 國際一般資料保護規範GDPR

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course builds statistical capacity (Target 17.19) by teaching 'data science core techniques' and 'data processing', which are essential for measuring progress on all SDGs and enhancing data availability (Target 17.18).
evidence: 資料科學核心技術專題
